skill.name: setup-pre-commit
skill.description: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged (Prettier), type checking, and tests in the current repo. Use when user wants to add pre-commit hooks, set up Husky, configure lint-staged, or add commit-time formatting/typechecking/testing.
skill.summary: Configure Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged, Prettier, type checks, and tests.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command patterns and one weak cryptography blocker. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive because the line only describes commit-hook setup. The external command instructions are legitimate for this DevOps skill, but they modify repository configuration and install or run local tooling, so users should review changes before use.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Modifying Pre-Commit Setup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to initialize Husky and create a pre-commit hook that runs lint-staged, type checking, and tests. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it changes repository configuration and causes commands to run during future commits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command instructions are explicit and semantically relevant to the skill. They appear legitimate, but they create executable commit-time behavior in the user's repository.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm, but that line is the skill description for commit-time formatting, type checking, and testing. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API use is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive metadata only. I found no evidence of cryptographic logic or weak algorithm use in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: The reviewed skill content does not contain override language, fake system instructions, claims of pre-approval, or instructions to skip security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file was reviewed with line numbers. No suspicious prompt-injection phrasing was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to run package-manager and project-script commands as part of setup and verification. These commands are normal for this task, but they can execute local project scripts and should be reviewed before running in untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly lists command lines to execute. The commands are purpose-aligned, but the execution surface is real.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when formatting, type errors, and failing tests reach commits. This skill configures Husky, lint-staged, Prettier, and optional project checks for consistent commit-time validation.
actual_capabilities: Detects common JavaScript package managers from lockfiles.
Installs Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier as development dependencies.
Initializes Husky and creates a pre-commit hook.
Configures lint-staged to format staged files with Prettier.
Adds a default Prettier configuration when none exists.
Verifies the hook setup with a lint-staged run.
limitations: Designed for JavaScript and TypeScript repositories with package.json.
Does not define missing typecheck or test scripts.
Requires package installation and local command execution.
May need manual adjustment for monorepos or custom package managers.

use_cases.title: Standardize Commits in a Team Repository
use_cases.description: Add automatic formatting and project checks before commits to reduce review noise.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New TypeScript Project
use_cases.description: Configure commit-time formatting, type checking, and tests early in a project.

use_cases.title: Improve Open Source Contribution Quality
use_cases.description: Give contributors clear automated checks before they submit changes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when a project needs standard pre-commit checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged and Prettier for this repository.

prompt_templates.title: Setup With Existing Scripts
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when the project already has test and typecheck scripts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a pre-commit hook that runs lint-staged first, then the existing typecheck and test scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Package Manager Aware Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when the repository may use npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun.
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect the package manager from lockfiles, then set up Husky, lint-staged, and Prettier using that tool.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Harden Existing Hooks
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for an existing repository with partial hook configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the current pre-commit setup, preserve existing behavior, and add lint-staged, Prettier, typecheck, and test steps where appropriate.

output_examples.input: Set up pre-commit hooks in a new npm project.
output_examples.output: The assistant installs the required dev dependencies, initializes Husky, creates the pre-commit hook, adds lint-staged formatting, and verifies the setup.

output_examples.input: Add commit checks but skip missing scripts.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks package.json, includes only scripts that exist, and explains which checks were omitted.

output_examples.input: Bring an existing repository onto a consistent formatting workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant preserves existing configuration where possible and adds missing lint-staged and Prettier files.

best_practices: Review package.json scripts before enabling them in a pre-commit hook.
Run the hook locally before committing the generated configuration.
Keep pre-commit checks fast enough for frequent developer use.
anti_patterns: Do not run unknown project scripts from untrusted repositories without review.
Do not overwrite existing formatter or hook configuration without comparing it first.
Do not add long integration test suites to every pre-commit run.

faq.question: Does this skill work with pnpm, yarn, or bun?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks common lockfiles and adapts package-manager commands when possible.

faq.question: Will it create typecheck or test scripts?
faq.answer: No. It only adds those commands to the hook when matching scripts already exist.

faq.question: Does it overwrite existing Prettier settings?
faq.answer: No. It creates a default Prettier configuration only when one is missing.

faq.question: Is this safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The setup runs package-manager commands and can execute local project scripts.

faq.question: What does lint-staged do here?
faq.answer: It formats staged files with Prettier before the commit is created.

faq.question: Can this be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
